summary.title: Example Threat Model Embedded Device
summary.description: This is an example threat model to demonstrate the STRIDE methodology.

diagrams.title: Context diagram

data.name: Embedded Device_01
data.description: This is a generic embedded device with a touch display and a USB interface for data exchange and an ethernet interface for cloud connectivity.

data.name: User
data.description: This actor represents all user roles, except the service engineer, who use the functions of the device.

data.name: VisioNize Cloud
data.description: This actor represents the VisioNize platform that provides additional digital features like over-the-air updates, remote monitoring and opt-in AI features.

data.name: Service Engineer
data.description: This actor represent the service engineer user who repairs and maintains the device.

data.name: Production
data.description: The actor represents all natural persons and processes that interact with the device during manufacturing.

diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the overall context of the device.

diagrams.title: Data flow diagram 01 - Operation/Maintenance

data.name: Firmware Updater
data.description: The software updater process includes all processes and services required for updating firmware on a device, including verification of files and installing.

threats.title: Install corrupted firmware
threats.description:
An attacker may try to deploy manipulated firmware on the devices for various reasons. i.e sabotage, espionage, cryptomining or data theft.
CVSS:4.0/AV:P/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N
threats.mitigation:
RCM-3 - Verify firmware update file
RCM-4 Verify system integrity

data.name: Service Interface
data.description: The service interface provides system access with a higher level of authority to override safety systems for maintenance and repair tasks.

threats.title: Hi-jack service access authority
threats.description: An attacker may use the higher privilege of the service access to execute unintended functions or access restricted paths.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Device Controller
data.description: The device controller process includes all functions and services that are used to control the device, i.e setting parameters, starting and stopping operation, controlling motors and actuators.

threats.title: Fake complaint for wrong device usage
threats.description: An entity may instruct the device controller to do things that are not recommended or that lead to damage to the customers property and later deny doing it.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Bypass user restriction
threats.description: A user may try to bypass restrictions imposed by user management, i.e to change parameters of a programmed procedure when this is reserved for admin users.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: User
data.description: This user is assumed to be present in front of the system.

threats.title: Unauthorized usage of device
threats.description:
An unauthorized entity may try to use or abuse the device by acting as a legitimate user.
CVSS:4.0/AV:P/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N
threats.mitigation: RCM-1 - Provide user management for roles and permissions control

data.name: VisioNize Cloud
data.description: This actor represents all services provided by the visionize platform.

threats.title: Login credentials are stolen
threats.description: The login credentials of the service engineer may be compromised.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Transfer software updates
data.description: This data flow includes the authentication of the device in the cloud, including a subsequent transfer of an update file to the device.

threats.title: MitM Attack on cloud connection
threats.description: an attacker may try to act as a mitm to the cloud and manipulate information sent to the cloud or received from it.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: DoS on device
threats.description: An attacker may try to use the cloud connectivity to launch a denial of service attack on the devices of a customer.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Control device functions
data.description: this data flow represents the interactions of a user standing in front of the device using the touch display to trigger device actions.

data.name: Read and write system data
data.description: This data flow includes all data that is written to the file system as a result of device controls.

data.name: Manage software update
data.description: This data flow includes storing the firmware file on the file system and also any operations to it except what is done by the bootloader.

data.name: Trigger device update
data.description: This data flow includes the firmware update trigger done from inside the service interface.

data.name: Execute service functions
data.description: The service interface can directly interact with the file system to execute service functions

threats.title: Abuse of service function calls
threats.description: An attacker may use the service interface to flood the system with service function requests via the remote service login.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Trigger device update
data.description: This data flow is triggered when a user executes the software  update feature on the touch display.

threats.title: Corrupted update stick
threats.description:
An attacker may try to disable or damage the device by triggering a USB update with a crafted update file, i.e. the filename may contain bad words that break the update function.
CVSS:4.0/AV:P/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N
threats.mitigation: RCM-2 - input validation for update file ingestion

data.name: File system
data.description: This is a general representation of the file system, that includes all memory except for the system log.

threats.title: Manipulate firmware
threats.description: An attacker may try to manipulate the firmware image on the embedded system, either during operation, boot or update.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: System penetration
threats.description: An unauthorized entity may try to read sensitive or confidential information from the file system.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Service Engineer
data.description: Service engineers are assumed to be eppendorf employees or authorized personnel.

threats.title: Fake service engineer role
threats.description: An attacker may spoof as a service engineer to abuse features and functions reserved for eppendorf personnel.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: System log
data.description: This data store represents the system log, which includes errors, device events and other data.

threats.title: Log tampering
threats.description:
An unauthorized entity may try to manipulate the system log to cover up their actions
CVSS:4.0/AV:P/AC:L/AT:N/PR:H/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N
threats.mitigation: RCM-5 Portect system log integrity

threats.title: Sensitive information leak
threats.description: An unauthorized entity may extract sensitive or confidential information from the system log
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

diagrams.description: This diagram covers data flow during operation and maintenance phase of the device

diagrams.title: Data flow diagram 02 - Production

data.name: Production
data.description: This actor includes personnel from production and, if applicable, automated data flows resulting from manufacturing.

data.name: Initial firmware installation
data.description: This data flow represents the initial commissioning of the device with firmware.

threats.title: installation of corrupted firmware
threats.description: During manufacturing, firmware is installed on the device, bypassing the regular updater process. This installation may already contain manipulated code.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

diagrams.description: This diagram covers data flows during production phase of the device.

diagrams.title: Data flow diagram 03 - Device Controls

data.name: Service access
data.description: This trust boundary encapsulated functions that are only available via service access.

data.name: User Management
data.description: This trust boundary represents the optional user management which can be used to restrict access to some device functions.

data.name: Coordinator
data.description: This process represents the central process that controls the authorization and execution of device functions.

data.name: System Log
data.reasonOutOfScope: This entity is covered in dfd 01

data.name: Control hardware
data.reasonOutOfScope: This is covered in the threat model of the device firmware.

data.name:
read and write 
to file system

labels:
read and write 
to file system

threats.title: Manipulated commands
threats.description:
A corrupted firmware may send unintended or dangerous commands to the actuators.
SAFETY RELEVANT!
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Deny access to device controls
threats.description: An attacker may try to deny control of the device by tampering with the data flowing to the device controller.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Program data
data.description: This data flow represents the program data being handled by the "Manage Programs" process.

threats.title: Malformed program data
threats.description: An attacker may be able to utilize malformed program data to force a crash of the system.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: User Data
data.description: This data flow represents the user data being handled by the "Manage User Data" process.

threats.title: Manipulate user privileges
threats.description: An attacker may manipulate the data flow to alter user privileges in an unintended way
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: Disrupt authentication
threats.description: An attacker may try to disrupt authentication of users and render the device unusable temporarily.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: File system
data.reasonOutOfScope: This entity is covered in dfd 01

data.name:
Manage 
Programs
data.description: This process covers CRUD operations on programs, i.e. a PCR cycle or a centrifuge run.

data.name:
Manage 
User Data
data.description: This process covers CRUD operations user data, i.e. name, role, etc.

data.name: Firmware
data.reasonOutOfScope: This is covered in the threat model of the device firmware.

data.name: User
data.description: This actor covers all allowed user roles: unauthenticated, regular, admin and service.

diagrams.description: This lvl 2 diagram further describes the device controller process from data fow diagram 01
